steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage explicitly states: ‘greater proximity alone can't explain the difference in park use.’ The question asks what point the text makes about the difference in park usage between the two cities. The answer must match this stated conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It was much larger than the researchers conducting the study expected.: The passage says nothing about what researchers expected to find. ✗
  • Choice B — It was calculated using sources that predate the survey.: Historical sources are never mentioned as a factor in the park use difference. ✗
  • Choice C — It could be due to inaccuracies in the survey results.: The passage does not suggest the data is flawed or inaccurate. ✗
  • Choice D — It is caused by something other than the parks' proximity to city residents.: Restates the passage's explicit conclusion word for word. ✓
That matches Choice D — It is caused by something other than the parks' proximity to city residents..
